pg45-p1-s2:
But we found that such codes and philosophies did not save us, no matter how much we
tried.
pg45-p1-s3:
We could wish to be moral, we could wish to be philosophically comforted, in fact, we could will these things
with all our might, but the needed power wasn't there.
pg45-p1-s4:
Our human resources, as marshalled by the will, were not
sufficient; they failed utterly.
pg45-p1:
would have recovered long ago. But we found that such codes and philosophies did not save us, no matter how much we
tried. We could wish to be moral, we could wish to be philosophically comforted, in fact, we could will these things
with all our might, but the needed power wasn't there. Our human resources, as marshalled by the will, were not
sufficient; they failed utterly.
pg45-p2-s1: Lack of power, that was our dilemma.
pg45-p2-s2:
We had to find a power by which we could live, and it had to be a Power greater
than ourselves. Obviously.
pg45-p2-s3: But where and how were we to find this Power?
pg45-p2:
Lack of power, that was our dilemma. We had to find a power by which we could live, and it had to be a Power greater
than ourselves. Obviously. But where and how were we to find this Power?
pg45-p3-s1: Well, that's exactly what this book is about.
pg45-p3-s2:
Its main object is to enable you to find a Power greater than yourself
which will solve your problem.
pg45-p3-s3: That means we have written a book which we believe to be spiritual as well as moral.
pg45-p3-s4:
And
it means, of course, that we are going to talk about God.
pg45-p3-s6:
Many times we talk to a
new man and watch his hope rise as we discuss his alcoholic problems and explain our fellowship.
pg45-p3-s7:
But his face falls when
we speak of spiritual matters, especially when we mention God, for we have re-opened a subject which our man thought he
had neatly evaded or entirely ignored.
pg45-p3:
Well, that's exactly what this book is about. Its main object is to enable you to find a Power greater than yourself
which will solve your problem. That means we have written a book which we believe to be spiritual as well as moral. And
it means, of course, that we are going to talk about God. Here difficulty arises with agnostics. Many times we talk to a
new man and watch his hope rise as we discuss his alcoholic problems and explain our fellowship. But his face falls when
we speak of spiritual matters, especially when we mention God, for we have re-opened a subject which our man thought he
had neatly evaded or entirely ignored.
pg45-p4-s2: We have shared his honest doubt and prejudice.
pg45-p4-s3: Some of us have been violently anti-religious.
pg45-p4-s4:
To
others, the word "God" brought up a particular idea of Him with which someone had tried to impress them during
childhood.
pg45-p4-s5: Perhaps we rejected this particular conception because it seemed inadequate.
pg45-p4-s6:
With that rejection we imagined
we had abandoned the God idea entirely.
pg45-p4:
We know how he feels. We have shared his honest doubt and prejudice. Some of us have been violently anti-religious. To
others, the word "God" brought up a particular idea of Him with which someone had tried to impress them during
